primary_users_or_beneficiaries: Researchers, labs, and research support teams
what_the_project_does: Document intelligence workspace using RAG and hybrid retrieval to explore large research corpora with transparent evidence and citations.
why_it_matters_to_nyu: Creates reusable research infrastructure for evidence-grounded discovery across large document collections.
work_completed_to_date: Core workbench is live; portfolio entry, live demo URL, and positioning are established.
current_output_or_demo: Live research workbench with citation tracing and evidence-grounded retrieval
next_30_day_goal: Add real screenshots, usage proof points, and a tighter demo narrative for broader internal visibility.
support_needed_from_supervisor: Support visibility, identify pilot groups, and help decide what adoption metrics should be tracked.
funding_case_for_leadership: Additional funding would support productization, onboarding, evaluation, and deployment across more research groups.
notes: Strong flagship project for making the case that applied AI infrastructure work is already delivering usable systems

primary_users_or_beneficiaries: Humanities researchers, archivists, and digital scholarship teams
what_the_project_does: AI-assisted exploration of early English books using translation, semantic indexing, and retrieval to support digital humanities research.
why_it_matters_to_nyu: Shows that the same AI infrastructure can support serious humanities research, not only technical domains.
work_completed_to_date: Live analysis environment exists and is represented in the portfolio with a public-facing description.
current_output_or_demo: Live analysis studio for archival and corpus exploration
next_30_day_goal: Add real screenshots and short humanities-focused use cases that explain research value clearly.
support_needed_from_supervisor: Confirm preferred framing for humanities audiences and whether any additional context is needed for supervisors.
funding_case_for_leadership: Additional funding would help extend AI tooling into more disciplines and strengthen the case for cross-school research infrastructure.

primary_users_or_beneficiaries: Research groups, labs, faculty, and institutional leadership
what_the_project_does: Platform mapping generative AI projects, use cases, and capabilities across NYU research groups.
why_it_matters_to_nyu: Provides institutional visibility into who is building what, where there is overlap, and where shared capabilities can be coordinated.
work_completed_to_date: Private preview concept, portfolio positioning, and request-access framing are in place.
current_output_or_demo: Private preview platform and network map of NYU GenAI activity
next_30_day_goal: Finalize access workflow, determine who should be invited first, and prepare a limited demo path.
support_needed_from_supervisor: Approval on external messaging, early-access process, and which groups should be included first.
funding_case_for_leadership: Additional funding would support platform maturation, data stewardship, onboarding, and broader institutional coordination around GenAI work.
notes: High strategic value because it helps leadership see and coordinate distributed AI activity

primary_users_or_beneficiaries: Faculty, staff, and research support teams
what_the_project_does: Hands-on workshops helping faculty and staff use generative AI tools for research, teaching, and workflow automation.
why_it_matters_to_nyu: Builds institutional adoption capacity so AI platforms and tools are actually used effectively.
work_completed_to_date: Workshop/training work is active and represented with a reference URL in the portfolio.
current_output_or_demo: Workshop and training system with institutional enablement focus
next_30_day_goal: Add workshop outcomes, participation data, and examples of workflow impact.
support_needed_from_supervisor: Help determine what metrics or outcomes should be captured for reporting upward.
funding_case_for_leadership: Additional funding would expand AI literacy, increase adoption, and turn one-off workshops into a scalable enablement program.
notes: Important support layer for all platform work

primary_users_or_beneficiaries: Public audiences, faculty-facing outreach, and communications stakeholders
what_the_project_does: Public-facing discovery experience for curated puppet performances with direct venue and ticket links.
why_it_matters_to_nyu: Demonstrates ability to ship polished audience-facing tools, not only internal research systems.
work_completed_to_date: Public site is live and included in the portfolio.
next_30_day_goal: Add screenshot and a short note about audience, engagement, or communications value.
support_needed_from_supervisor: Decide whether to keep it in the main portfolio or frame it as a special public-facing project.
funding_case_for_leadership: Additional funding rationale is weaker than the infrastructure projects, but it supports the case that the work also has external-facing communication value.

primary_users_or_beneficiaries: Public health researchers and policy-focused teams
what_the_project_does: Multimodal system detecting and classifying tobacco products from retail shelf images for policy and public health analysis.
why_it_matters_to_nyu: Shows applied AI capability in a domain with clear policy and health relevance.
work_completed_to_date: Private system and portfolio summary exist, but public-facing materials are limited.
current_output_or_demo: Private computer vision system with shelf detection and classification
next_30_day_goal: Clarify what can be shown publicly and prepare a sanitized screenshot or summary artifact.
support_needed_from_supervisor: Guidance on what can be shared publicly and whether this should be used in funding narratives now or later.
funding_case_for_leadership: Additional funding would support domain-specific applied AI work with public health relevance and strengthen the case for maintaining broad technical capability.
notes: Not yet ready for broad public visibility
